//_comment: A DocType JSON is the schema definition Frappe uses to auto-generate the MariaDB table (tabMaintenance Request), the desk UI form, list view, and permissions — all from this one file. You almost never hand-write these in real projects; you use the Frappe UI's 'New DocType' screen and it generates this JSON for you. It's shown here so you can see exactly what that generates.
name: Maintenance Request
//_is_submittable: Makes this a 'submittable' document (Draft -> Submitted -> Cancelled), like a Sales Invoice. Enables the on_submit hook we wired up in hooks.py.
//_autoname: Auto-generates readable IDs like MR-2026-00001 instead of raw hashes.

fields.label: Asset Name
fields.//_reqd: reqd=1 means required. Frappe enforces this both client-side (form won't save) and server-side (DB insert blocked) automatically.

fields.label: Assigned Technician
fields.//_link_field: A Link fieldtype is Frappe's foreign key. This stores the `name` (primary key) of an Asset Technician row — this is exactly how ERPNext wires up e.g. Sales Invoice -> Customer.

fields.label: Status
fields.//_read_only: Status is driven by the Workflow engine (see maintenance_workflow.json), not typed directly by users.

permissions.//_note: Regular employees can raise + submit requests, but can't delete or cancel — that stays with System Manager. This mirrors ERPNext's standard role-based permission pattern.
